description: Enforce server-side validation that /peer/key-rotation rejects requests where newAgentId does not match the agent ID derived from newPublicKey, preserve valid rotation behavior, add regression coverage, and verify the SDK and repo test suite still pass.

userStories.title: Locate the authoritative key rotation validation path
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to identify the exact /peer/key-rotation handler and any helper functions it uses so that the binding fix is implemented in the authoritative validation path.
acceptanceCriteria: The SDK source file and function handling /peer/key-rotation are identified
The code location where newAgentId and newPublicKey can be validated before state mutation is documented in the task output or implementation notes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The authoritative SDK route is `registerPeerRoutes()` in `packages/agent-world-sdk/src/peer-protocol.ts`, specifically the `fastify.post("/peer/key-rotation", ...)` handler. It already decodes `oldIdentity.publicKeyMultibase` and `newIdentity.publicKeyMultibase` to `oldPublicKeyB64` and `newPublicKeyB64`, then validates the old binding with `agentIdFromPublicKey(oldPublicKeyB64) !== agentId`. The correct insertion point for the upcoming new-key binding check is in that same handler immediately after multibase decoding and before any path can reach `peerDb.upsert(agentId, newPublicKeyB64, {})`.

userStories.title: Identify and reuse the agent ID derivation helper
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to confirm the existing helper that derives an agent ID from a public key so that the fix reuses the source-of-truth identity binding logic instead of duplicating it.
acceptanceCriteria: The existing helper used to derive an agent ID from a public key is identified in the SDK or shared identity code
The planned validation path references the existing helper instead of introducing duplicate derivation logic
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The existing source-of-truth helper is `agentIdFromPublicKey()` in `packages/agent-world-sdk/src/crypto.ts`. The authoritative `/peer/key-rotation` handler in `packages/agent-world-sdk/src/peer-protocol.ts` already imports that helper, so the upcoming new-key binding validation should call `agentIdFromPublicKey(newPublicKeyB64)` in that handler immediately after multibase decoding and before any path can reach `peerDb.upsert(agentId, newPublicKeyB64, {})`.

userStories.title: Add derived new-agent validation in the rotation handler
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the /peer/key-rotation handler to derive the expected agent ID from newPublicKey and compare it to newAgentId so that mismatched rotated identities are rejected.
acceptanceCriteria: The handler derives an expected agent ID from newPublicKey using the existing helper
The handler compares the derived agent ID to newAgentId before accepting the rotation
Requests with matching newAgentId and newPublicKey continue into the existing success path
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Return a stable 400 error for mismatched rotated identities
userStories.description: As a client developer, I need invalid rotation requests to fail with a consistent HTTP 400 response so that a mismatched new identity binding is easy to detect and diagnose.
acceptanceCriteria: A request where derived(newPublicKey) does not equal newAgentId returns HTTP 400
The response body includes the exact message "newAgentId does not match newPublicKey"
The mismatch response is produced by the server-side validation path rather than by a downstream failure
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Guard state mutation behind the new binding validation
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need the new binding check to run before any key rotation persistence or membership updates so that rejected requests cannot mutate server state.
acceptanceCriteria: The newAgentId/newPublicKey validation executes before any rotation state mutation or persistence
A mismatched rotation request does not update stored keys, rotation records, or membership state
Existing success-path state updates still occur for valid rotation requests
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add a regression test for mismatched new key binding
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a focused regression test that submits a malformed rotation request so that future changes cannot reintroduce acceptance of mismatched rotated identities.
acceptanceCriteria: A new or updated SDK test submits a /peer/key-rotation request where newAgentId does not match the ID derived from newPublicKey
The test asserts that the response status is 400
The test asserts that the response body includes "newAgentId does not match newPublicKey"
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Confirm valid key rotation behavior still succeeds
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to verify that correctly bound rotation requests still complete successfully so that the security fix does not break legitimate key rotation.
acceptanceCriteria: Existing valid key rotation test coverage still passes or is updated only for validation ordering
A correctly bound newAgentId and newPublicKey continues to produce a successful rotation response
No valid rotation test weakens its assertions to accommodate the fix
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build the SDK package after the handler and test changes
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need the agent-world SDK package to build cleanly after the change so that the fix remains compatible with the TypeScript project configuration.
acceptanceCriteria: Running npm --prefix packages/agent-world-sdk run build completes successfully
No new TypeScript build errors are introduced by the handler or test changes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Run repository tests covering key rotation behavior
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need the repository test suite to pass after the fix so that the new validation is verified against current integration expectations.
acceptanceCriteria: Running node --test test/*.test.mjs completes successfully
Existing key rotation-related tests continue to pass alongside the new regression coverage
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Review final scope for a minimal security fix
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need to confirm the delivered change remains limited to binding validation, stable error handling, and regression coverage so that unrelated protocol behavior is not modified.
acceptanceCriteria: The final change set is limited to key rotation validation, error handling, and regression coverage for newAgentId/newPublicKey binding
No unrelated transport, discovery, membership, or broadcast behavior is modified
Typecheck passes
